 " there be that poor fool , Vixen , whimpering for I at home , " say Bartle . 
 " I can never bring she here with I for fear she should be strike with 
 Mrs. Poyser 's eye , and the poor bitch might go limp for ever after . " 
 " I have   never any need to drive Gyp back , " say Adam , laugh . " he always 
 turn back of his own head when he find out I be come here . " 
 " aye , aye , " say Bartle . " a terrible woman ! - make of needle , make of 
 needle . but I stick to Martin - I shall always stick to Martin . and 
 he like the needle , God help he ! he be a cushion make on purpose for 
 them . " 
 " but she be a downright good - natur'd woman , for all that , " say Adam , 
 " and as true as the daylight . she be a bit cross wi ' the dog when they 
 offer to come in th ' house , but if they depend on she , she would take care 
 and have them well feed . if her tongue 's keen , her heart 's tender : I have 
 see that in time o ' trouble . she be one o ' those woman as be well 
 than their word . " 
 " well , well , " say Bartle , " I do not   say th ' apple be not   sound at the 
 core ; but it set my tooth on edge - it set my tooth on edge . " 
 Chapter LIV 
 the Meeting on the Hill 
 ADAM understand Dinah 's haste to go away , and draw hope rather than 
 discouragement from it . she be fearful lest the strength of her feeling 
 towards he should hinder she from wait and listen faithfully for 
 the ultimate guide voice from within . 
 " I wish I have ask she to write to I , though , " he think . " and yet even 
 that might disturb she a bit , perhaps . she want to be quite quiet 
 in her old way for a while . and I have   no right to be impatient and 
 interrupt she with my wish . she be tell I what her mind be , 
 and she be not a woman to say one thing and mean another . I will wait 
 patiently . " 
 that be Adam 's wise resolution , and it throve excellently for the first 
 two or three week on the nourishment it get from the remembrance of 
 Dinah 's confession that Sunday afternoon . there be a wonderful amount 
 of sustenance in the first few word of love . but towards the middle 
 of October the resolution begin to dwindle perceptibly , and show 
 dangerous symptom of exhaustion . the week be unusually long : Dinah 
 must surely have have more than enough time to make up her mind . let a 
 woman say what she will after she have once tell a man that she love 
 he , he be a little too flushed and exalt with that first draught she 
 offer he to care much about the taste of the second . he tread the 
 earth with a very elastic step as he walk away from she , and make 
 light of all difficulty . but that sort of glow die out : memory get 
 sadly dilute with time , and be not strong enough to revive we . Adam 
 be no long so confident as he have be . he begin to fear that perhaps 
 Dinah 's old life would have too strong a grasp upon she for any new 
 feeling to triumph . if she have not feel this , she would surely have 
 write to he to give he some comfort ; but it appear that she hold 
 it right to discourage he . as Adam 's confidence wan , his patience 
 wan with it , and he think he must write himself . he must ask Dinah 
 not to leave he in painful doubt long than be needful . he sit up 
 late one night to write she a letter , but the next morning he burn it , 
 afraid of its effect . it would be bad to have a discouraging answer 
 by letter than from her own lip , for her presence reconcile he to she 
 will . 
 you perceive how it be : Adam be hunger for the sight of Dinah , and 
 when that sort of hunger reach a certain stage , a lover be likely to 
 still it though he may have to put his future in pawn . 
 but what harm could he do by go to Snowfield ? Dinah could not be 
 displeased with he for it . she have not forbid he to go . she must 
 surely expect that he would go before long . by the second Sunday in 
 October this view of the case have become so clear to Adam that he be 
 already on his way to Snowfield , on horseback this time , for his hour 
 be precious now , and he have borrow Jonathan Burge 's good nag for the 
 journey . 
 what keen memory go along the road with he ! he have often be to 
 oakbourne and back since that first journey to Snowfield , but beyond 
 Oakbourne the greystone wall , the broken country , the meagre tree , 
 seem to be tell he afresh the story of that painful past which he 
 know so well by heart . but no story be the same to we after a lapse of 
 time - or rather , we who read it be no long the same interpreter - and 
 Adam this morning bring with he new thought through that grey 
 country , thought which give an altered significance to its story of the 
 past . 
 that be a base and selfish , even a blasphemous , spirit which rejoice 
 and be thankful over the past evil that have blight or crush another , 
 because it have be make a source of unforeseen good to ourselves . Adam 
 could never cease to mourn over that mystery of human sorrow which have 
 be bring so close to he ; he could never thank God for another 's 
 misery . and if I be capable of that narrow - sighted joy in Adam 's 
 behalf , I should still know he be not the man to feel it for himself . 
 he would have shake his head at such a sentiment and say , " Evil 's 
 evil , and sorrow 's sorrow , and you can not   alter it be natur by wrap 
 it up in other word . other folk be not create for my sake , that I 
 should think all square when thing turn out well for I . " 
 but it be not ignoble to feel that the full life which a sad 
 experience have bring we be worth our own personal share of pain . 
 surely it be not possible to feel otherwise , any more than it would be 
 possible for a man with cataract to regret the painful process by which 
 his dim blur sight of man as tree walk have be exchange for 
 clear outline and effulgent day . the growth of high feeling within 
 we be like the growth of faculty , bring with it a sense of add 
 strength . we can no more wish to return to a narrow sympathy than 
 a painter or a musician can wish to return to his cruder manner , or a 
 philosopher to his less complete formula . 
 something like this sense of enlarged be be in Adam 's mind this 
 Sunday morning , as he ride along in vivid recollection of the past . his 
 feeling towards Dinah , the hope of pass his life with she , have be 
 the distant unseen point towards which that hard journey from Snowfield 
 eighteen month ago have be lead he . tender and deep as his love 
 for Hetty have be - so deep that the root of it would never be tear 
 away - his love for Dinah be well and more precious to he , for it 
 be the outgrowth of that full life which have come to he from his 
 acquaintance with deep sorrow . " it be like as if it be a new strength to 
 I , " he say to himself , " to love she and know as she love I . I shall 
 look t ' she to help I to see thing right . for she be well than I 
 be - there be less o ' self in she , and pride . and it be a feeling as give 
 you a sort o ' liberty , as if you could walk more fearless , when you have 
 more trust in another than y ' have in yourself . I have   always be 
 think I know well than they as belong to I , and that be a poor 
 sort o ' life , when you can not   look to they near to you t ' help you 
 with a bit well thought than what you have   get inside you a'ready . " 
 it be more than two o'clock in the afternoon when Adam come in sight of 
 the grey town on the hill - side and look searchingly towards the green 
 valley below , for the first glimpse of the old thatched roof near the 
 ugly red mill . the scene look less harsh in the soft October sunshine 
 than it have in the eager time of early spring , and the one grand charm 
 it possess in common with all wide - stretch woodless region - that 
 it fill you with a new consciousness of the overarch sky - have a 
 milder , more soothing influence than usual , on this almost cloudless 
 day . Adam 's doubt and fear melt under this influence as the delicate 
 weblike cloud have gradually melt away into the clear blue above he . 
 he seem to see Dinah 's gentle face assure he , with its look alone , 
 of all he long to know . 
 he do not expect Dinah to be at home at this hour , but he get down from 
 his horse and tie it at the little gate , that he might ask where she 
 be go to - day . he have set his mind on follow she and bring she 
 home . she be go to Sloman 's end , a hamlet about three mile off , over 
 the hill , the old woman tell he - have set off directly after morning 
 chapel , to preach in a cottage there , as her habit be . anybody at the 
 town would tell he the way to Sloman 's end . so Adam get on his horse 
 again and ride to the town , put up at the old inn and take a 
 hasty dinner there in the company of the too chatty landlord , from whose 
 friendly question and reminiscence he be glad to escape as soon as 
 possible and set out towards Sloman 's end . with all his haste it be 
 nearly four o'clock before he could set off , and he think that as 
 Dinah have go so early , she would perhaps already be near return . 
 the little , grey , desolate - look hamlet , unscreene by shelter 
 tree , lie in sight long before he reach it , and as he come near he 
 could hear the sound of voice sing a hymn . " perhaps that be the last 
 hymn before they come away , " Adam think . " I will walk back a bit and 
 turn again to meet she , far off the village . " he walk back till he 
 get nearly to the top of the hill again , and seat himself on a loose 
 stone , against the low wall , to watch till he should see the little 
 black figure leave the hamlet and wind up the hill . he choose 
 this spot , almost at the top of the hill , because it be away from all 
 eye - no house , no cattle , not even a nibble sheep near - no presence 
 but the still light and shadow and the great embrace sky . 
 she be much long come than he expect . he wait an hour at 
 least watch for she and thinking of she , while the afternoon shadow 
 lengthen and the light grow soft . at last he see the little black 
 figure come from between the grey house and gradually approach the 
 foot of the hill . slowly , Adam think , but Dinah be really walk at 
 her usual pace , with a light quiet step . now she be begin to wind 
 along the path up the hill , but Adam would not move yet ; he would not 
 meet she too soon ; he have set his heart on meet she in this assured 
 loneliness . and now he begin to fear lest he should startle she too 
 much . " yet , " he think , ' she be not one to be overstartle ; she be always 
 so calm and quiet , as if she be prepare for anything . " 
 what be she think of as she wind up the hill ? perhaps she have find 
 complete repose without he , and have cease to feel any need of his 
 love . on the verge of a decision we all tremble : hope pause with 
 flutter wing . 
 but now at last she be very near , and Adam rise from the stone wall . 
 it happen that just as he walk forward , Dinah have pause and turn 
 round to look back at the village - who do not pause and look back in 
 mount a hill ? Adam be glad , for , with the fine instinct of a lover , 
 he feel that it would be good for she to hear his voice before she 
 see he . he come within three pace of she and then say , " Dinah ! " she 
 start without look round , as if she connect the sound with no 
 place . " Dinah ! " Adam say again . he know quite well what be in her 
 mind . she be so accustomed to think of impression as purely spiritual 
 monition that she look for no material visible accompaniment of the 
 voice . 
 but this second time she look round . what a look of yearn love it 
 be that the mild grey eye turn on the strong dark - eyed man ! she do 
 not start again at the sight of he ; she say nothing , but move towards 
 he so that his arm could clasp her round . 
 and they walk on so in silence , while the warm tear fall . Adam be 
 content , and say nothing . it be Dinah who speak first . 
 " Adam , " she say , " it be the Divine Will . my soul be so knit to yours 
 that it be but a divide life I live without you . and this moment , now 
 you be with I , and I feel that our heart be fill with the same 
 love . I have a fulness of strength to bear and do our heavenly Father 's 
 will that I have lose before . " 
 Adam pause and look into her sincere eye . 
 " then we will never part any more , Dinah , till death part we . " 
 and they kiss each other with a deep joy . 
 what great thing be there for two human soul than to feel that they 
 be join for life - to strengthen each other in all labour , to rest on 
 each other in all sorrow , to minister to each other in all pain , to be 
 one with each other in silent unspeakable memory at the moment of the 
 last parting ? 
 Chapter LV 
 Marriage Bells 
 in little more than a month after that meeting on the hill - on a rimy 
 morning in depart November - Adam and Dinah be marry . 
 it be an event much thought of in the village . all Mr. Burge 's man have 
 a holiday , and all Mr. Poyser 's , and most of those who have a holiday 
 appear in their good clothe at the wedding . I think there be hardly 
 an inhabitant of Hayslope specially mention in this history and still 
 resident in the parish on this November morning who be not either in 
 church to see Adam and Dinah marry , or near the church door to greet 
 they as they come forth . Mrs. Irwine and her daughter be wait at 
 the churchyard gate in their carriage   for they have a carriage now   to 
 shake hand with the bride and bridegroom and wish they well ; and in the 
 absence of Miss Lydia Donnithorne at Bath , Mrs. Best , Mr. Mills , and 
 Mr. Craig have feel it incumbent on they to represent " the family " at the 
 Chase on the occasion . the churchyard walk be quite line with familiar 
 face , many of they face that have first look at Dinah when she 
 preach on the Green . and no wonder they show this eager interest on 
 her marriage morning , for nothing like Dinah and the history which have 
 bring she and Adam Bede together have be know at Hayslope within the 
 memory of man . 
 Bessy Cranage , in her neat cap and frock , be cry , though she do 
 not exactly know why ; for , as her cousin Wiry Ben , who stand near she , 
 judiciously suggest , Dinah be not go away , and if Bessy be in low 
 spirit , the good thing for she to do be to follow Dinah 's example and 
 marry an honest fellow who be ready to have she . next to Bessy , just 
 within the church door , there be the Poyser child , peep round 
 the corner of the pew to get a sight of the mysterious ceremony ; 
 Totty 's face wear an unusual air of anxiety at the idea of see 
 cousin Dinah come back look rather old , for in Totty 's experience no 
 married people be young . 
 I envy they all the sight they have when the marriage be fairly end 
 and Adam lead Dinah out of church . she be not in black this morning , 
 for her Aunt Poyser would by no mean allow such a risk of incur bad 
 luck , and have herself make a present of the wedding dress , make all of 
 grey , though in the usual Quaker form , for on this point Dinah could not 
 give way . so the lily face look out with sweet gravity from under 
 a grey Quaker bonnet , neither smile nor blushing , but with lip 
 tremble a little under the weight of solemn feeling . Adam , as he 
 press her arm to his side , walk with his old erectness and his head 
 throw rather backward as if to face all the world well . but it be 
 not because he be particularly proud this morning , as be the will not of 
 bridegroom , for his happiness be of a kind that have little reference 
 to man 's opinion of it . there be a tinge of sadness in his deep joy ; 
 Dinah know it , and do not feel aggrieved . 
 there be three other couple , follow the bride and bridegroom : 
 first , Martin Poyser , look as cheery as a bright fire on this rimy 
 morning , lead quiet Mary Burge , the bridesmaid ; then come Seth serenely 
 happy , with Mrs. Poyser on his arm ; and last of all Bartle Massey , with 
 Lisbeth - Lisbeth in a new gown and bonnet , too busy with her pride in 
 her son and her delight in possess the one daughter she have desire 
 to devise a single pretext for complaint . 
 Bartle Massey have consent to attend the wedding at Adam 's earnest 
 request , under protest against marriage in general and the marriage of a 
 sensible man in particular . nevertheless , Mr. Poyser have a joke against 
 he after the wedding dinner , to the effect that in the vestry he have 
 give the bride one more kiss than be necessary . 
 behind this last couple come Mr. Irwine , glad at heart over this good 
 morning 's work of join Adam and Dinah . for he have see Adam in the 
 bad moment of his sorrow ; and what well harvest from that painful 
 seed - time could there be than this ? the love that have bring hope and 
 comfort in the hour of despair , the love that have find its way to the 
 dark prison cell and to poor Hetty 's dark soul - this strong gentle 
 love be to be Adam 's companion and helper till death . 
 there be much shake of hand mingle with " God bless you 's " and other 
 good wish to the four couple , at the churchyard gate , Mr. Poyser 
 answering for the rest with unwonted vivacity of tongue , for he have 
 all the appropriate wedding - day joke at his command . and the woman , he 
 observe , could never do anything but put finger in eye at a wedding . 
 even Mrs. Poyser could not trust herself to speak as the neighbour 
 shake hand with she , and Lisbeth begin to cry in the face of the very 
 first person who tell she she be get young again . 
 Mr. Joshua Rann , have a slight touch of rheumatism , do not join 
 in the ringing of the bell this morning , and , look on with some 
 contempt at these informal greeting which require no official 
 co - operation from the clerk , begin to hum in his musical bass , " oh what 
 a joyful thing it be , " by way of prelude a little to the effect he 
 intend to produce in the wedding psalm next Sunday . 
 " that be a bit of good news to cheer Arthur , " say Mr. Irwine to his 
 mother , as they drive off . " I shall write to he the first thing when we 
 get home . " 
 epilogue 
 it be near the end of June , in 1807 . the workshop have be shut 
 up half an hour or more in Adam Bede 's timber - yard , which use to 
 be Jonathan Burge 's , and the mellow evening light be fall on the 
 pleasant house with the buff wall and the soft grey thatch , very much 
 as it do when we see Adam bring in the key on that June evening 
 nine year ago . 
 there be a figure we know well , just come out of the house , and shade 
 her eye with her hand as she look for something in the distance , for 
 the ray that fall on her white borderless cap and her pale auburn hair 
 be very dazzling . but now she turn away from the sunlight and look 
 towards the door . 
 we can see the sweet pale face quite well now : it be scarcely at all 
 alter - only a little full , to correspond to her more matronly 
 figure , which still seem light and active enough in the plain black 
 dress . 
 " I see he , Seth , " Dinah say , as she look into the house . " let we go 
 and meet he . come , Lisbeth , come with Mother . " 
 the last call be answer immediately by a small fair creature with 
 pale auburn hair and grey eye , little more than four year old , who run 
 out silently and put her hand into her mother 's . 
 " Come , Uncle Seth , " say Dinah . 
 " aye , aye , we be come , " Seth answer from within , and presently 
 appear stoop under the doorway , be tall than usual by the 
 black head of a sturdy two - year - old nephew , who have cause some delay by 
 demand to be carry on uncle 's shoulder . 
 " well take he on thy arm , Seth , " say Dinah , look fondly at the 
 stout black - eyed fellow . " he be troublesome to thee so . " 
 " Nay , nay : Addy like a ride on my shoulder . I can carry he so for a 
 bit . " a kindness which young Addy acknowledge by drum his heel 
 with promising force against Uncle Seth 's chest . but to walk by Dinah 's 
 side , and be tyrannize over by Dinah 's and Adam 's child , be Uncle 
 Seth 's earthly happiness . 
 " where didst see he ? " ask Seth , as they walk on into the adjoining 
 field . " I can not   catch sight of he anywhere . " 
 " between the hedge by the roadside , " say Dinah . " I see his hat and his 
 shoulder . there he be again . " 
 " trust thee for catch sight of he if he be anywhere to be see , " say 
 Seth , smile . " thee't like poor mother use to be . she be always on 
 the look out for Adam , and could see he soon than other folk , for 
 all her eye get dim . " 
 " he be be long than he expect , " say Dinah , take Arthur 's watch 
 from a small side pocket and look at it ; " it be nigh upon seven now . " 
 " Aye , they would have a deal to say to one another , " say Seth , " and the 
 meeting ' ud touch them both pretty closish . why , it be get on towards 
 eight year since they part . " 
 " yes , " say Dinah , " Adam be greatly move this morning at the thought 
 of the change he should see in the poor young man , from the sickness he 
 have undergo , as well as the year which have change we all . and the 
 death of the poor wanderer , when she be come back to we , have be 
 sorrow upon sorrow . " 
 " see , Addy , " say Seth , lower the young one to his arm now and 
 pointing , " there be Father come - at the far stile . " 
 Dinah hasten her step , and little Lisbeth run on at her utmost speed 
 till she clasp her father 's leg . Adam pat her head and lift she 
 up to kiss she , but Dinah could see the mark of agitation on his face 
 as she approach he , and he put her arm within his in silence . 
 " well , youngster , must I take you ? " he say , try to smile , when Addy 
 stretch out his arm - ready , with the usual baseness of infancy , to 
 give up his Uncle Seth at once , now there be some rare patronage at 
 hand . 
 " it be cut I a good deal , Dinah , " Adam say at last , when they be 
 walk on . 
 " Didst find he greatly alter ? " say Dinah . 
 " why , he be alter and yet not alter . I should ha ' know he anywhere . 
 but his colour 's change , and he look sadly . however , the doctor say 
 he will soon be set right in his own country air . he be all sound in th ' 
 inside ; it be only the fever shatter he so . but he speak just the 
 same , and smile at I just as he do when he be a lad . it be wonderful 
 how he be always have just the same sort o ' look when he smile . " 
 " I have   never see he smile , poor young man , " say Dinah . 
 " but thee wilt see he smile , to - morrow , " say Adam . " he ask after 
 thee the first thing when he begin to come round , and we could talk to 
 one another . ' I hope she be not   alter , ' he say , ' I remember her face 
 so well . ' I tell he ' no , ' " Adam continue , look fondly at the eye 
 that be turn towards his , " only a bit plumper , as thee'dst a right 
 to be after seven year . ' I may come and see she to - morrow , may not   I ? ' he 
 say ; ' I long to tell she how I have   think of she all these year . ' " 
 " Didst tell he I would always use the watch ? " say Dinah . 
 " Aye ; and we talk a deal about thee , for he say he never see a woman 
 a bit like thee . ' I shall turn Methodist some day , ' he say , ' when she 
 preach out of door , and go to hear she . ' and I say , ' Nay , sir , you 
 can not   do that , for Conference have forbid the woman preach , and she be 
 give it up , all but talk to the people a bit in their house . ' " 
 " ah , " say Seth , who could not repress a comment on this point , " and a 
 sore pity it be o ' conference ; and if Dinah have see as I do , we would ha ' 
 leave the Wesleyans and join a body that ' ud put no bond on Christian 
 liberty . " 
 " nay , lad , nay , " say Adam , ' she be right and thee wast wrong . there be 
 no rule so wise but what it be a pity for somebody or other . Most o ' 
 the woman do more harm nor good with their preaching - they have   not get 
 Dinah 's gift nor her sperrit - and she be see that , and she think it 
 right to set th ' example o ' submitting , for she be not hold from other 
 sort o ' teaching . and I agree with she , and approve o ' what she do . " 
 Seth be silent . this be a stand subject of difference rarely 
 allude to , and Dinah , wish to quit it at once , say , " Didst 
 remember , Adam , to speak to Colonel Donnithorne the word my uncle and 
 aunt entrust to thee ? " 
 " yes , and he be go to the Hall Farm with Mr. Irwine the day after 
 to - morrow . Mr. Irwine come in while we be talk about it , and he

 say - and he be in the right of it - as it will be bad for he t ' have his 
 feeling stir with see many people one after another . ' we must

 have he home again . " 
 Adam be silent a little while , and then say , " it be very cutting when

 " there be a sort of wrong that can never be make up for . " ' " 
 " why , there be Mr. and Mrs. Poyser come in at the yard gate , " say 
 Seth . 
 " so there be , " say Dinah . " Run , Lisbeth , run to meet Aunt Poyser . come 
 in , Adam , and rest ; it have be a hard day for thee . "
